Title: Palmitoleic acid as a coordinating molecule between the invasive pinewood nematode and its newly associated fungi

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: life-stage expression profiling from RNA-seq dataset
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in adult stages, particularly females
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with adult/female TAG metabolism context but not functionally validated as causal in this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as adult-high lipid metabolism gene; not directly manipulated.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. dgat-2 expression is relatively high in adult PWNs, especially females. 2. The pattern suggests a role in TAG accumulation during reproductive stages. 3. Direct causal evidence for palmitoleic-acid-mediated fecundity was not provided.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; qRT-PCR; GC/MS fatty acid profiling; stable-isotope labeling with 31D-C16:0 to detect 29D-C16:1; fecundity assay (egg laying)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: monounsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis; delta-9 desaturation of saturated fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI SRA PRJNA798902; BioProject PRJNA941926); metabolomics (study data included in article/supplement)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in adults than many other lipid genes; higher in females than males
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: fatty acid desaturation / palmitoleic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in PWNs cultured on Sporothrix sp.1 versus Ophiostoma ips; relatively enriched in female adults
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: female nematodes / whole nematode lipid stores
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a delta-9 double bond into palmitic acid (C16:0) to form palmitoleic acid (C16:1)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fat-5 is the sole delta-9 fatty acid desaturase identified in B. xylophilus and is implicated in MUFA biosynthesis. 2. fat-5 expression is higher in female adults than males, matching higher C16:1 levels in females. 3. fat-5 RNAi lowers C16:1 abundance and significantly reduces female egg laying, supporting a causal role in palmitoleic-acid-dependent fecundity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although fat-5 was downregulated in PWNs feeding on Sporothrix sp.1 versus O. ips, PWNs still accumulated more C16:1 due largely to dietary acquisition from the fungus; the gene nonetheless supports endogenous synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to suppress pinewood nematode reproduction by RNAi or inhibitor-based control of endogenous C16:1 production; could be combined with manipulation of fungal nutrient environment.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes endogenous C16:1 production and enhances female fecundity/egg laying
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown reduces fecundity; broader lipid homeostasis effects likely but not detailed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Culture on Sporothrix sp.1 versus Ophiostoma ips; supplementation with C16:0 or C16:1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Coordinates reproduction/fecundity of pinewood nematode within symbiosis

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling under fungal diet comparison
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis from monounsaturated fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in Sporothrix sp.1-treated PWN versus O. ips-treated PWN
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: monounsaturated fatty acids such as C16:1/C18:1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely introduces an additional double bond to convert MUFAs toward PUFAs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fat-2.2 expression was strongly upregulated in Sporothrix sp.1-fed PWNs. 2. The gene is discussed as converting MUFAs to PUFAs. 3. Its induction supports altered fatty acid remodeling in response to the fungal diet, but direct causal validation for fecundity was not performed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among experimentally profiled lipid genes, but not functionally validated by RNAi in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased MUFA-to-PUFA conversion under Sporothrix sp.1 feeding
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Culture on Sporothrix sp.1 versus Ophiostoma ips

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling under fungal diet comparison
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis from monounsaturated fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in Sporothrix sp.1-treated PWN versus O. ips-treated PWN
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: monounsaturated fatty acids such as C16:1/C18:1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely introduces an additional double bond to convert MUFAs toward PUFAs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fat-2.3 expression was strongly upregulated in Sporothrix sp.1-fed PWNs. 2. The gene is discussed as participating in conversion of MUFAs to PUFAs. 3. It supports diet-induced fatty acid remodeling but was not directly shown to control fecundity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Profiled as part of pathway context rather than a primary functional target.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased MUFA-to-PUFA conversion under Sporothrix sp.1 feeding
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Culture on Sporothrix sp.1 versus Ophiostoma ips

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; qRT-PCR; promoter motif analysis of fat-5; GC/MS fatty acid profiling; fecundity assay
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential positive regulator of endogenous C16:1 biosynthesis in female PWNs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: monounsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis and reproduction-related lipid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: nuclear hormone receptor; HNF4-like DNA-binding factor
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible positive feedback at reproductive stage via C16:1 accumulation, but direct autoregulatory loop for nhr-80 not established
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Culture on different fungal diets; sex-specific adult stage; C16:1/C18:1 supplementation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nhr-80 is highly expressed in adult, especially female, PWNs. 2. The fat-5 promoter contains a putative HNF4-binding motif, and nhr-80 knockdown reduces fat-5 expression and C16:1 content. 3. nhr-80 RNAi markedly decreases female egg laying, indicating that nhr-80 promotes fecundity through activating endogenous palmitoleic acid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising molecular target for suppressing pinewood nematode reproduction by disrupting lipid-metabolic regulation; may be suitable for RNAi-based pest control
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental stage; sex (female-enriched); possibly dietary lipid status
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Coordinates nematode reproduction within the nematode-fungus symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in adults, particularly females
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: female nematodes / whole nematode lipid pool
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown reduces egg production; broader endocrine/metabolic effects possible but not detailed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C16:1 accumulation and female egg-laying capacity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study explicitly positions nhr-80 upstream of fat-5 based on homology to C. elegans and RNAi results.
